opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head is splitting, my stomach is in knots, and I'm so wiped out I can't get off the couch. I've been smiling for their brochures all week while they ignore everything I actually say, and now I feel like I've completely crashed.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been building for three days and got really bad after a big work event — the headache won't shift even with ibuprofen, I feel sick and dizzy when I stand up, and I need to know if something is actually wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: built up over the last three days, much worse in the last day after a big corporate event I was heavily involved in
symptom_narrative.duration: about 72 hours and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: a tight, pressing, band-like headache across my forehead and the back of my head and neck, with my stomach churning and cramping
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the headache is really bad and constant, and I feel too exhausted and nauseous to do anything
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all across my forehead, the back of my head and my neck, and a churning cramping feeling in my stomach
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: pretty much constant since it started, worse when I try to get up and do things
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying still in a quiet dark room takes the edge off a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up, moving around, and anything to do with work
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: nausea and stomach cramps, feeling completely drained, and I get dizzy and have to sit back down when I stand up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had stress headaches and a dodgy stomach during rough patches at work before, but never this bad or this floored
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been basically bedbound, can't work, can't concentrate, and can barely stand long enough to make a drink

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: occasional stress headaches and stomach upset during busy work periods
past_medical_history.how_managed: usually just rest and over-the-counter painkillers

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: a couple of tablets over the day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'm the only person from my background on my team and I've become the unofficial face of the company's diversity push — this week I was used heavily for a big marketing campaign. I'm constantly expected to represent and to outperform, I feel unheard by leadership, and I'm exhausted from holding myself together and not being able to just be myself at work. There's a work award on my desk I've turned face down.

objective_data_offered.value: I don't have a thermometer at home, but I don't feel hot or feverish
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported, no device — cannot exclude fever

objective_data_offered.value: I've been sipping water and I can keep small amounts down, but the nausea makes it hard to drink or eat much
when I stand up I go really lightheaded and have to sit straight back down
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: symptom description only; no home blood-pressure device to check for a postural drop
